# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

const handler = async (request: Request): Promise<Response> => {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const target = url.searchParams.get("url");

  if (!target) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Missing url query parameter" }), {
      status: 400,
      headers: { "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8" },
    });
  }

  try {
    await Deno.permissions.request({ name: "net" as Deno.PermissionDescriptor["name"] });

    const parsed = new URL(target);
    if (parsed.protocol !== "http:" && parsed.protocol !== "https:") {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Only http and https URLs are supported" }), {
        status: 400,
        headers: { "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8" },
      });
    }

    const upstream = await fetch(parsed.toString(), {
      headers: {
        "user-agent": "Deno-Deploy-Fetcher/1.0",
        "accept": request.headers.get("accept") ?? "*/*",
      },
    });

    const contentType = upstream.headers.get("content-type") ?? "text/plain; charset=utf-8";
    const body = await upstream.arrayBuffer();

    return new Response(body, {
      status: upstream.status,
      headers: {
        "content-type": contentType,
        "x-upstream-status": String(upstream.status),
      },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: "Failed to fetch URL",
        detail: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
      }),
      {
        status: 500,
        headers: { "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8" },
      },
    );
  }
};

Deno.serve(handler);
